title: FindLaw Workspace
content: A task and project management platform I designed for FindLaw's internal teams. I handled the information architecture and UX — dashboard layout, task tracking, search, and the data visualization layer. The work went from wireframes through usability testing to a final spec handed off to engineering.

images.alt: Balsamiq render of Dashboard for FindLaw Workspace
images.caption: Dashboard Interface: A wireframe of the main dashboard, organized into four widgets: Alerts, My Tasks, Team's Projects, and My Projects. The sidebar is collapsible and the widget layout is user-configurable via a hamburger menu.

images.alt: Balsamiq render Summary Page for FindLaw Workspace
images.caption: Data-Driven Summary Dashboard: A conceptual wireframe showing the same four widgets rendered as charts — bar, line, pie, and horizontal bar. This was an exploratory direction for turning the task tables into a high-level summary view.

images.alt: Screenshot of Tasks Page for FindLaw Workspace
images.caption: Task Management: The My Tasks view, showing a sortable table with priority, due date, and account columns. Tasks are color-coded by urgency, with Complete and Details actions on each row and a quick filter bar at the top.

images.alt: Screenshot of Search Page for FindLaw Workspace
images.caption: Search Functionality: The search interface, where users can build and save custom queries. A left panel manages saved searches; the main panel lets users define and edit filter parameters with dropdown menus and input fields.

images.alt: Screenshot of Empty Tasks Page FindLaw Workspace
images.caption: Empty State Handling: The My Tasks page with no active tasks. Shows the full column structure and filter controls in context — the empty state was a deliberate design decision to keep the interface readable rather than hiding controls until data appears.
